summary: This week in AI, significant advancements have been made in various sectors. China's leading AI companies are ramping up pressure on Silicon Valley, while OpenAI faces challenges from Apple's lawsuit. NVIDIA is maximizing intelligence per dollar for post-training workloads, and enterprises are struggling to measure AI infrastructure costs.
insight: The increasing competition between the US and China in the AI sector is driving innovation, but also raises concerns about data security and the potential for AI models to be used for malicious purposes.
highlights: China's Moonshot AI releases Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter open-weight model, challenging US AI dominance.
OpenAI faces a trade secrets lawsuit from Apple, potentially disrupting its IPO plans.
NVIDIA's Vera Rubin maximizes intelligence per dollar for post-training workloads, a key metric for agentic AI.

articles.title: China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance

articles.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
articles.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of  turning AI into a business.

articles.title: Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
articles.description: OpenAI shares lessons from deploying long-running AI models, highlighting new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.

articles.title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
articles.description: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics.

articles.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use

articles.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
articles.description: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

articles.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Maximizes Intelligence per Dollar for Post-Training Workloads — a Key Metric for Agentic AI
articles.description: Lowest cost per token from extreme codesign maximizes intelligence per dollar for post-training in the agentic era.

articles.title: A scorecard for the AI age
articles.description: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

articles.title: US public health agencies to test OpenAI and Anthropic AI models
